commit.message:
Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive (#48037)

* Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive

The Form block was unusable when the contact-form module was active but
the blocks module was not. Three barriers prevented it from working:

1. The parent jetpack/contact-form block was only registered via the
   Blocks module's extension loader. Now registered directly from the
   Forms package in Contact_Form_Plugin::register_contact_form_blocks().

2. The editor JS depended on jetpack-blocks-editor, a script only
   registered by the Blocks module. Without it, WordPress silently
   skipped loading the Forms editor bundle entirely.

3. The JS availability check (Jetpack_Editor_Initial_State) and module
   status check (useModuleStatus) both defaulted to "unavailable" when
   the Blocks module didn't provide the initial state, showing an
   "Activate Forms" placeholder instead of the actual block.

The fix registers a minimal jetpack-blocks-editor script with the
required initial state (available_blocks, modules, feature_flags) when
the Blocks module is inactive, allowing Forms to work independently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore the block registration in the Blocks extension so

commit.message:
Fix gh attestation verify using mutually exclusive flags (#82)

Remove --signer-repo flag from gh attestation verify invocations in both
ProvenanceVerifier.cs and install-copilotd.sh. The gh CLI treats
--signer-repo and --signer-workflow as mutually exclusive flags in the
same flag group (cert-identity, cert-identity-regex, signer-repo,
signer-workflow). Using both causes attestation verification to always
fail with: 'if any flags in the group [...] are set none of the others
can be'.

Keep --signer-workflow as it is more specific (constrains both the repo
and the exact workflow file) and provides stronger provenance guarantees.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] perf: precompute score maps in beerAverages, beerStdDevs and per-taster correlations (#92)

* perf: precompute score maps in beerAverages, beerStdDevs and per-taster correlations

Replace repeated O(B×S) and O(T×S) linear scans in five Result functions
with a single Map.ofList pass over the scores list, followed by O(log B)
or O(log T) map lookups inside the per-beer / per-taster loops.

Functions optimised:
- beerAverages: builds Map<beerId, Score list> once before iterating beers
- beerStandardDeviations: same Map reuse
- correlationToAverages: builds both Map<beerId,…> and Map<tasterName,…>
- correlationToAbv: builds Map<tasterName, Score list> once
- correlationToAbvPrice: same Map reuse

Complements the existing open PR that optimised correlationBetweenTasters
and correlationToAge with the same technique.

All 51 tests pass.

Optimize export: use JPEG frames + reuse compositions + faster temp files

Three performance improvements to the export pipeline:

1. Source frame loading: use JPEG frames from the recording session's
   .frames/ directory via VideoFrameReader instead of creating a new
   MediaClip + MediaComposition per frame. This eliminates per-frame
   MP4 file open/parse/seek overhead (the single biggest bottleneck).

2. Fallback extraction: when .frames/ is unavailable, create the source
   MediaComposition once and reuse it for all frame seeks, instead of
   recreating it per frame.

3. Temp file format: save composited frames as JPEG (0.92 quality)
   instead of lossless PNG. JPEG encoding is significantly faster and
   produces smaller files, reducing both I/O and encoding time.

Both the MP4 export (VideoEncoder) and GIF export (ExportEngine) paths
are optimized. A FallbackExtractFrameAsync method is kept as a
last-resort for edge cases where both the frame reader and composition
fail.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): use http launch profile to prevent HTTPS binding failure in CI

Aspire defaults to the https profile when available, setting
ASPNETCORE_URLS from its primary URL only (HTTPS). This means the
app never binds the HTTP port, so the HTTP health check gets
'Connection refused'. Explicitly select the 'http' launch profile for
both API and web so Aspire binds HTTP only — no dev cert needed.

Added 'http' profile to Waymarked.Web launchSettings (kept existing
'Waymarked.Web' profile with HTTPS for direct dotnet run usage).

feat: HW/SW PTP auto-detection in inferno-configure.sh

After install/configure, detect whether the node's NIC supports hardware
PTP timestamping and inject 'hardware-clock = /dev/ptpN' into
statime-inferno.toml only when a real device node is confirmed.

- sysfs path: /sys/class/net/<NIC>/device/ptp/ (primary, fastest)
- ethtool -T fallback: also scans /sys/class/ptp/ by device symlink
  to find the actual /dev/ptpN device even when sysfs path fails
- Software-only nodes: no hardware-clock entry → statime uses virtual
  clock (safe, ~500 µs offset, Dante works fine)
- Hardware PTP nodes: hardware-clock = "/dev/ptpN" → statime opens the
  char device directly → AsyncClient sends immediate initial overlay →
  no timeout risk in plugin_prepare regardless of convergence time

Also removes the ExecStartPre sed strip from statime-inferno.service:
that line was a workaround that prevented hardware-clock from ever
being used. inferno-configure.sh is now the authoritative source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE to statime capability set for audio

Without CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE, statime (running as root with restricted
CapabilityBoundingSet) cannot send Unix datagrams to client sockets
owned by the 'core' user. This silently dropped all usrvclock overlay
updates, causing inferno-bridge plugin_prepare to time out with
'no clock available (timeout waiting for overlay update)' every restart.

Root cause: commit b7fdc4c tightened the bounding set but omitted
CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE, which root needs to write to user-owned socket files
(permissions 0755, not world-writable).

The permanent code fix is in usrvclock-rs (client socket now 0o777),
but this service-level fix is also needed until that build is deployed.

Live fix already applied via drop-in on nodes .46, .43, .51.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add cross-platform self-update support for Linux/macOS (#81)

* Add cross-platform self-update support for Linux/macOS

Extend the self-update system to work on Linux and macOS, not just Windows.

Key changes:
- ProvenanceVerifier: Add gh attestation verify for non-Windows provenance
  checking (Authenticode remains for Windows). Uses --signer-repo and
  --source-ref to constrain trust to the DamianEdwards/copilotd repo on main.
- GitHubReleaseService: Add GetPlatformAssetName() for cross-platform asset
  resolution (linux/osx tar.gz, win zip). Add tar.gz extraction with proper
  timeout handling and Unix file permission preservation.
- UpdateService: Remove Windows-only gates. Platform-aware binary naming
  (copilotd vs copilotd.exe), chmod +x on staged/installed binaries, archive
  attestation verification before extraction, SIGINT-based graceful shutdown
  on Unix (vs shutdown-instance helper on Windows).
- RunCommand: Remove Windows-only gate on update loop. Add Unix
  SpawnUpdateInstaller using setsid for session detachment (matching
  StartCommand pattern).
- UpdateCommand: Remove Windows-only gate, update descriptions.

Closes #44

commit.message:
Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive (#48037)

* Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive

The Form block was unusable when the contact-form module was active but
the blocks module was not. Three barriers prevented it from working:

1. The parent jetpack/contact-form block was only registered via the
   Blocks module's extension loader. Now registered directly from the
   Forms package in Contact_Form_Plugin::register_contact_form_blocks().

2. The editor JS depended on jetpack-blocks-editor, a script only
   registered by the Blocks module. Without it, WordPress silently
   skipped loading the Forms editor bundle entirely.

3. The JS availability check (Jetpack_Editor_Initial_State) and module
   status check (useModuleStatus) both defaulted to "unavailable" when
   the Blocks module didn't provide the initial state, showing an
   "Activate Forms" placeholder instead of the actual block.

The fix registers a minimal jetpack-blocks-editor script with the
required initial state (available_blocks, modules, feature_flags) when
the Blocks module is inactive, allowing Forms to work independently.

commit.message:
EXP-2629: Per-patient Hill fitting, ODC validation, sticky hypers

Round 5 of EGP phase separation research:

Hill Fitting: Fit per-patient Hill suppression curves from fasting
windows. Hill K varies 150x across patients (0.1-15.0U), confirming
massive heterogeneity in hepatic insulin sensitivity. However, R² is
low (0.001-0.081) — fasting glucose_roc is too noisy for reliable
parameter identification. Correction recovery slopes remain the
better per-patient EGP estimator.

ODC Validation: IOB@midnight → overnight drift replicates in 2/3 ODC
patients. odc-74077367 has the strongest correlation in the entire
study (r=-0.572). Finding is robust across Loop and AAPS systems,
bolusing and basal-only patients, and different carb logging behaviors.

Sticky Hypers: Episodes >180 mg/dL for ≥3h show 2.3-10.4x normal IOB
but glucose still rising 24-55% of the time. Patient e is most extreme
(+0.7 mg/dL/hr at 7U IOB, 55% rising). Population Hill model cannot
explain this — suggests per-patient hepatic or peripheral insulin
resistance not captured by current model.

Includes fig15-18 visualizations and updated comprehensive report.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(generator): inline first WHERE/HAVING condition with aligned AND/OR (#30)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (powered by Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements inline WHERE/HAVING clause formatting as specified in #29.

**Before:**
```sql
SELECT a, b
FROM my_table
WHERE
  x > 1
  AND y < 2
  OR z = 'foo'
```

**After:**
```sql
SELECT a, b
FROM my_table
WHERE x > 1
  AND y < 2
   OR z = 'foo'
```

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py`: Added `where_sql`, `having_sql`, and
`_inline_clause_sql` methods. The helper splits the connector output
line-by-line, tracks parenthesis depth to handle nested paren blocks,
and applies right-justified alignment so all condition content starts at
the same column (col 6 for WHERE, col 7 for HAVING).
- `tests/fixtures/select/where_clause.{input,expected}.sql`: New fixture
pair covering multiple AND/OR conditions.
- Updated 5 existing fixture snapshots to reflect the new format.
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md`: Updated WHERE/HAVING section and fixed 3
inline examples that showed the old format.

Resolves #29

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
